user.title: HearthLight
user.description: This installs the HearthLight theme, sets it as the backend default, registers the HearthLight dashboard cards, and creates the ready-made HearthLight dashboard. All behaviors can be toggled later in the integration's options.

data.set_default_theme: Set HearthLight as the backend default theme

data_description.manage_theme: Keep themes/hearthlight/hearthlight.yaml installed and up to date. Local edits to that file are overwritten on update.
data_description.set_default_theme: Re-applies HearthLight for light and dark on every Home Assistant start.
data_description.register_card_resource: Makes the HearthLight cards available on dashboards.
data_description.create_dashboard: Adds a ready-made HearthLight dashboard (Home, Spaces, System, Support) to the sidebar. It rebuilds itself as areas and devices change. To hide something from it, add a label named exactly 'hearthlight-exclude' to that area, device, or entity. To customize the layout, use the dashboard's edit menu and take control — HearthLight never overwrites a customized dashboard.
data_description.managed_users: Each selected user gets a remote-access switch and duration setting. Turning the switch on allows that user to log in remotely until the duration elapses; turning it off (or the timer expiring) restores local-only login and ends their active sessions.
data_description.provision_support_user: Creates a managed 'HearthLight Support' admin account owned by the integration. It stays locked (local-only, unknown password) until its remote-access switch is turned on, which generates a one-session password shown on the card. If the account is deleted it is recreated automatically; turning this off (or removing the integration) deletes it.
data_description.home_address: Street address of this home. The Support page's email card includes it in the subject line so support tickets identify the install. Leave empty to omit it.

themes_include_missing.title: HearthLight theme is not being loaded
themes_include_missing.description:
The theme file was installed, but Home Assistant is not loading it. Add a themes include to your configuration.yaml:

```yaml
frontend:
  themes: !include_dir_merge_named themes
```

Then restart Home Assistant.

no_ha_auth_provider.title: Support user could not be provisioned
no_ha_auth_provider.description: HearthLight could not create the Support user because this installation has no 'homeassistant' (username/password) auth provider. Your configuration.yaml customizes `auth_providers:` — add the `homeassistant` provider back, or turn off 'Provision the HearthLight Support user' in the integration options.

yaml_mode_resource.title: Dashboard resource could not be registered automatically
yaml_mode_resource.description:
Your dashboards are configured in YAML mode, so HearthLight loaded its cards globally instead. For a cleaner setup, add the resource to your Lovelace YAML config:

```yaml
lovelace:
  resources:
    - url: /hearthlight/hearthlight-brand-card.js
      type: module
```

dashboard_create_failed.title: HearthLight dashboard could not be created automatically
dashboard_create_failed.description:
HearthLight could not create its dashboard through Home Assistant's dashboard storage. You can create it manually: go to Settings > Dashboards > Add dashboard, create a new dashboard, then open its raw configuration editor and replace the contents with:

```yaml
strategy:
  type: custom:hearthlight
```
